headline: Third Preparatory Meeting of BRICS Women's Working Group convened by the Ministry of Women and Child Development
fullText:
Ministry of Women and Child Development

Third Preparatory Meeting of BRICS Women's Working Group convened by the Ministry of Women and Child Development

BRICS Member Countries hold discussions on strengthening collaboration and building consensus ahead of the BRICS Women Track Meetings in Kochi, Kerala from 6-9 July 2026

29 JUN 2026 9:53PM by PIB Delhi

The Ministry of Women and Child Development convened the Third Preparatory Meeting of the BRICS Women Track under India's BRICS Chairship in virtual mode on 29th June 2026 under the theme Building for Resilience, Innovation, Cooperation and Sustainability. The meeting was chaired by Shri Anil Malik, Secretary, Ministry of Women and Child Development, who underscored the significance of the meeting in preparing for the BRICS Women Working Group Meeting and the BRICS Women Ministerial Meeting to be held in Kochi, Kerala, from 6-9 July 2026.

The meeting focused on advancing discussions on the priority areas and key outcomes, including the Digital Repository of Best Practices and the BRICS Women’s Capacity Building Guidelines. BRICS Member Countries shared their valuable inputs and reaffirmed their commitment to collaborative and consensus-based outcomes.

Congratulating India on its BRICS Chairship, Member Countries appreciated the progress made under the Women Track and expressed their continued support for strengthening cooperation on women's empowerment.

The Chair thanked all participating delegations for their constructive contributions and reiterated India's commitment to fostering practical partnerships that promote women-led development across BRICS nations.

(Release ID: 2279228)
summaryText:
### OVERVIEW

The Ministry of Women and Child Development convened the Third Preparatory Meeting of the BRICS Women Track on June 29, 2026, to strengthen collaboration and build consensus ahead of the BRICS Women Track Meetings in Kochi, Kerala, from July 6-9, 2026. 

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS

* The meeting theme was "Building for Resilience, Innovation, Cooperation and Sustainability".
* Priority areas and key outcomes included the Digital Repository of Best Practices and the BRICS Women’s Capacity Building Guidelines.
* BRICS Member Countries shared their valuable inputs and reaffirmed their commitment to collaborative and consensus-based outcomes.
* Member Countries congratulated India on its BRICS Chairship and expressed their continued support for strengthening cooperation on women's empowerment.
* The meeting was held under India’s BRICS Chairship in virtual mode.
category: Ministry of Women and Child Development

headline: Union Minister Shri Shivraj Singh Chouhan to Visit Haryana tomorrow
fullText:
Ministry of Agriculture &

Union Minister Shri Shivraj Singh Chouhan to Visit Haryana tomorrow

Shri Shivraj Singh Chouhan to Attend the Concluding Ceremony of the 'Khet Bachao Abhiyan', Interact Directly with Farmers

Bawal in Haryana's Rewari district to host the concluding event of the month-long nationwide campaign

29 JUN 2026 9:35PM by PIB Delhi

Union Minister for Agriculture & Farmers' Welfare and Rural Development, Shri Shivraj Singh Chouhan, will visit Bawal in Rewari district of Haryana on June 30 to attend the main concluding ceremony of the 'Khet Bachao Abhiyan', where he will interact directly with farmers, agricultural scientists, and experts.

The concluding programme of the 'Khet Bachao Abhiyan' will be held on the morning of June 30 in Bawal, Rewari. Shri Chouhan will reach Bawal at 9:30 AM. Upon his arrival, he will first visit an exhibition showcasing agriculture-related models and stalls featuring modern farming practices, soil health management, crop protection, animal husbandry, and rural livelihood initiatives.

Following the exhibition visit, the Union Minister will participate as the Chief Guest in the concluding ceremony of the 'Khet Bachao Abhiyan'. The programme will include detailed discussions on the campaign's experiences, successful initiatives, and the roadmap ahead. During his address and interaction, Shri Chouhan will engage with farmers on issues related to farm protection, reducing crop losses, promoting organic and scientific farming practices, and various rural development initiatives, with the objective of ensuring that the benefits of government programmes reach farmers directly and effectively.

Haryana Agriculture Minister Shri Shyam Singh Rana, along with scientists, officials, and experts from various agricultural institutions, will also participate as speakers during the event. They will present the efforts undertaken and the model activities implemented during the 'Khet Bachao Abhiyan'. The programme will feature presentations and discussions on soil testing, crop diversification, improved seed varieties, irrigation management, livestock protection, and welfare schemes of the Central and State Governments available for farmers. Local farmers will also share their experiences, enabling successful agricultural practices adopted in the region to be replicated in other districts.

(Release ID: 2279210)

Read this release in:
summaryText:
### OVERVIEW
Union Minister Shri Shivraj Singh Chouhan will visit Bawal in Rewari district, Haryana, on June 30 to attend the concluding ceremony of the 'Khet Bachao Abhiyan', a nationwide campaign to promote modern farming practices and protect farms from crop losses.

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS
* The 'Khet Bachao Abhiyan' campaign will conclude in Bawal, Rewari district, Haryana, on June 30.
* Union Minister Shri Shivraj Singh Chouhan will attend the concluding ceremony as the Chief Guest.
* He will interact directly with farmers, agricultural scientists, and experts during the event.
* Chouhan will arrive at Bawal at 9:30 AM on June 30.
* The event will feature an exhibition showcasing agriculture-related models and stalls on modern farming practices, soil health management, and rural livelihood initiatives.
* The programme will cover topics such as soil testing, crop diversification, improved seed varieties, irrigation management, livestock protection, and welfare schemes available for farmers.

headline: Union Minister Shri Shivraj Singh Chouhan to Visit Flood-Hit Areas of Arunachal Pradesh on July 1
fullText:
Ministry of Agriculture &

Union Minister Shri Shivraj Singh Chouhan to Visit Flood-Hit Areas of Arunachal Pradesh on July 1

Union Minister Shri Shivraj Singh Chouhan to Chair High-Level Review Meeting in Guwahati on June 30

Two-day Northeast visit by Union Minister Shri Shivraj Singh Chouhan on the directions of Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi

29 JUN 2026 9:33PM by PIB Delhi

On the directions of Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi, Union Minister for Agriculture & Farmers' Welfare and Rural Development, Shri Shivraj Singh Chouhan, will undertake a two-day visit to the Northeast. Shri Chouhan will depart from New Delhi on June 30 and hold a high-level review meeting in Guwahati, Assam, the same day. On July 1, he will visit the flood-affected areas of Arunachal Pradesh to assess the situation on the ground.

Under the guidance of Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi, the Central Government is closely monitoring the flood situation in the affected regions and is committed to accelerating relief and rehabilitation efforts through on-ground assessment and prompt decision-making. Taking serious note of the flood situation in Assam and Arunachal Pradesh, the Prime Minister has directed Union Minister Shri Shivraj Singh Chouhan to undertake an immediate field visit to review the situation firsthand.

As per the scheduled programme, Shri Chouhan will leave New Delhi on June 30 and arrive in Guwahati, where he will chair a high-level meeting with senior officials of the Assam Government. The meeting will review the impact of the floods on crops, villages, and critical infrastructure in Assam and adjoining areas, assess the progress of ongoing relief operations, and discuss the requirements for further assistance and intervention.

Following the review in Assam, Shri Chouhan will visit the flood-affected areas of Arunachal Pradesh on July 1. He will undertake both aerial and ground surveys to assess the condition of flood-hit villages, agricultural fields, and relief camps. During the visit, the Union Minister will interact directly with affected farmers, local residents, women's groups, and youth to understand the effectiveness of the relief measures undertaken so far and identify the immediate additional support required.

Later in the day, Shri Chouhan will also hold meetings with Ministers of the Arunachal Pradesh Government, senior officials, and other public representatives. The discussions will focus on assessment of crop losses, compensation and crop insurance payments for affected farmers, restoration of flood-damaged roads, bridges, and embankments, as well as strategies for rebuilding rural infrastructure.

(Release ID: 2279209)

Read this release in:
summaryText:
### OVERVIEW
Union Minister Shri Shivraj Singh Chouhan is set to visit flood-hit areas of Arunachal Pradesh on July 1 following a two-day visit to the Northeast, at the direction of Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi. The visit aims to assess the flood situation and enable expedited relief and rehabilitation efforts.

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS
- Union Minister Shri Shivraj Singh Chouhan to depart from New Delhi on June 30 for a two-day visit to the Northeast.
- High-level review meeting in Guwahati, Assam, on June 30 to review flood impact and relief operations.
- Visit to flood-affected areas of Arunachal Pradesh on July 1 for assessment and interaction with affected residents.
- Meetings with Arunachal Pradesh Government ministers, officials, and public representatives on July 1 to discuss relief strategies.
